summary: First iteration full backend + frontend regression. Created /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covering 25 cases across auth, seed, services, fare, bookings (create/list/accept/complete), driver requests/earnings/KYC, wallet topup, AI chat & parse-booking, admin stats/users/bookings/promo, fleet add-vehicle/stats, and RBAC 401/403. After fixing one ObjectId serialization bug in POST /api/admin/promo, 25/25 backend tests pass. Frontend smoke-tested end-to-end via Playwright: OTP login, all 4 quick-demo tiles redirect to correct portal, customer home renders 9 service tiles, BookRide flow (map + pickup/drop + popular places + get-estimates), Wallet topup +100 (verified), AI Assistant chat returns reply (Emergent LLM key working), Profile save UI, Admin Mission Control with stats + chart, Fleet portal with stats & add-vehicle list. All critical flows green.

issues: Recharts emits 'width(-1) height(-1)' warning on first paint because ResponsiveContainer measures before the right-pane has a height. Chart still renders correctly after layout. Consider setting an explicit min-height on the chart wrapper to silence the warning.

The Accept button (data-testid=accept-{id}) sits next to Skip but in screenshots its label appears poorly visible against the flame background — consider verifying text color/contrast in production build.

action_items: (Optional) Silence Recharts width/height(-1) warning on /admin overview by giving the chart wrapper an explicit min-height (h-72 already set but ResponsiveContainer fires before measurement).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 744 lines — fine for MVP but consider splitting routers (auth/bookings/driver/admin/fleet/ai) into separate modules as features grow.

Multiple Mongo insert handlers correctly use `{**rec}` to avoid `_id` mutation; POST /api/admin/promo was the only place that didn't. Fixed by spreading the dict on insert and pop('_id') before returning. Apply this pattern consistently to any new insert handler.
Auth uses HS256 JWT with no expiry checked on /api/auth/me path beyond signature validity — fine for demo, but add `exp` claim verification for production.
OTP delivery is correctly stubbed (returned in response) and gated by demo emails; ensure this short-circuit is removed before production.

Frontend uses process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL via axios `api` helper — good. All interactive elements carry data-testid attributes (verified on Login, BookRide, Wallet, AI, Profile, Driver, Admin, Fleet).
test_credentials: customer@rkpooja.test / driver@rkpooja.test / admin@rkpooja.test / fleet@rkpooja.test — OTP 123456 (demo mode). Seeded by POST /api/seed/demo (idempotent).
seed_data_creation: POST /api/seed/demo creates demo users + 1 customer + 1 driver + 1 admin + 1 fleet account. Tests run autouse seed fixture. Tests created a few TEST_PROMO* codes and one fleet vehicle BR99XX0001 — not cleaned up (acceptable for demo data).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression file lives at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. Re-run via: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. Tests are interdependent (session-scoped login + a shared created_booking fixture flows requested→accepted→completed). Demo OTP is hardcoded to 123456 for *@rkpooja.test emails. Promo serialization bug already fixed.
rca of the issue: POST /api/admin/promo returned HTTP 500 with `TypeError: 'ObjectId' object is not iterable / vars() argument must have __dict__`. Root cause: handler did `await db.promos.insert_one(rec); return rec`. PyMongo mutates the inserted dict by adding `_id: ObjectId(...)` in place, so FastAPI's jsonable_encoder then tried to serialize a raw bson ObjectId. Fix: insert a copy (`insert_one({**rec})`) then `rec.pop('_id', None)` defensively, and return the clean dict. Verified by re-running test_admin_promo_create_and_list → PASS, plus GET /api/admin/promos lists the new code.
